primary_text:
Most Australians have no idea how a startup actually gets funded.

Birchal is the rails. A retail investor reads an offer document, the platform handles the legals and the cap table, and the money moves to the founder. That is Crowd-Sourced Funding. It is how [PLACEHOLDER:total-raised-on-Birchal] has reached Australian companies through Birchal so far.

Now Birchal is running its own CSF campaign on the platform it built.

Follow the campaign to read the offer document the moment it goes live.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: How Australian startups actually get funded
description: Follow Birchal's CSF campaign. Read the offer when it opens.
_variant_note: Platform-as-product hook. Leads with mechanism (how CSF actually moves money). Audience: solution-aware retail investors who have seen Birchal campaigns but not invested. CTA is 'Follow campaign' (pre-offer-safe). Verbatim CSF warning preserved.
_compliance_findings_resolved: Rule 1 equal terms (s 738G): no VIP/exclusive/first-access language
Rule 2 pre-offer language (INFO 217): no 'invest', 'buy', 'subscribe' verbs; uses 'follow', 'read'
Rule 3 verbatim warning (s 738ZG short-form): pasted character-for-character from FarmThru/Birchal canonical line
Rule 4 cap and cooling-off: not in hero, will appear on offer page (acceptable for pre-campaign)
_compliance_findings_open_at_publish: PLACEHOLDER:total-raised-on-Birchal -- verify a public, citable Birchal aggregate (e.g. '$X raised across Y companies') before publishing. Replace tag or remove the sentence.
